targetUsers: Automation agencies with 10-50+ staff running 50-200+ daily workflow executions across n8n and other workflow engines
coreValueProposition: Automation OS is the execution governance layer for automation agencies. It separates workflow creation (technical, infrequent) from workflow execution (operational, frequent), providing a cockpit that gives non-technical staff safe, audited access to run automations without touching builder interfaces.
keyFeatures: Engine-agnostic task execution via Workflow Engine Adapter pattern (MVP: n8n Cloud and self-hosted)
Role-based access control with Permission Groups scoping which staff can execute which task categories
Persistent FIFO execution queue with 3 auto-retries, configurable timeouts, and duplicate prevention (5-minute cooldown per user per task)
Full execution audit trail with CSV export, admin error detail, and per-user history scoping
File upload and download per execution with cloud storage (R2/S3) and 30-day retention
Email notifications on execution completion with result link
Client workspace data model pre-built in MVP for seamless Phase 2 client portal delivery

userRoles.description: Platform-wide administrator. Manages organisations, monitors platform health, onboards new agency accounts. Operates across all tenants.
Full control within their organisation. Configures engines, creates and manages tasks, manages permission groups, views all execution history including full error detail, and uses test mode.
Executes tasks permitted by their permission groups. Views execution history for tasks they can access. Cannot configure tasks, engines, or permissions.
Executes tasks assigned to them via permission groups. Views only their own personal execution history. Read-only access beyond task execution.
PHASE 2. External client of the agency. Sees only tasks explicitly shared to their client workspace via a branded portal. Data model present in MVP; portal UI ships Phase 2.

businessRules: Organisations are provisioned exclusively by system_admin. There is no self-serve organisation registration. Once an organisation exists, org_admin invites staff via email invitation. Users cannot register independently.
Users join an organisation via an email invitation issued by org_admin. The invitation contains a one-time token. Accepting the invitation sets the user's password and activates their account.
A task must have status=active before it can be executed by manager or user roles. Tasks in draft or inactive status are not visible in the execution interface.

Duplicate execution prevention: a user cannot trigger the same task more than once within a 5-minute window. The system returns a clear rejection message with time-remaining when a duplicate is detected.

Each task has a configurable timeout (timeoutSeconds, default 300). If the workflow engine does not respond within this period, the execution is marked as status=timeout with a clear failure message. Three automatic retries are attempted for network failures before marking as failed.

The workflow engine powering a task is never exposed to manager or user roles. Staff see only the task name, description, input guidance, and expected output. Engine abstraction is enforced at the API layer.

Execution history visibility is role-scoped: system_admin sees all orgs, org_admin sees all executions within their org, manager sees executions for tasks in their accessible categories, user sees only their own executions.
Full error detail (engine response body, stack traces) is visible only to org_admin and system_admin. Manager and user roles see a sanitised error message.

File uploads associated with a task execution are stored in cloud storage (R2 or S3). Files are retained for 30 days from execution date. After expiry, files are deleted and download links are invalidated.

Permission groups grant users access to task categories. A user must belong to at least one permission group that includes a category to execute tasks within that category. A user may belong to multiple permission groups.
Task test mode is restricted to org_admin. Testing a task executes it against the live engine but records the result with a test flag, bypassing duplicate prevention and not triggering notifications.
When a workflow engine is deactivated or deleted, all tasks referencing that engine are also deactivated. Tasks cannot be activated without a connected, active engine.
The execution queue is persistent (survives server restarts). Workers process executions in FIFO order. Queue concurrency is configurable via environment variable.

organisations.notes: Organisations are the top-level tenant container. Created exclusively by system_admin. Org admins can update their own organisation settings.

users.notes: Users are created via invitation (create = send invite and accept invite flow). Org admins manage users within their organisation. System admins have cross-org visibility.

workflowEngines.notes: Workflow engine connections are managed exclusively by org_admin. MVP supports one n8n connection per organisation.

taskCategories.notes: Task categories are used for organisation and permission control. Managed by org_admin. Soft deleted; tasks with deleted category have categoryId set to the deleted category UUID (nullable).

tasks.notes: Tasks are execution units wrapping a workflow on a connected engine. Managed by org_admin. Staff see only active tasks they have permission to execute. Soft deleted.

permissionGroups.notes: Permission groups are managed by org_admin. They link users to task categories, controlling which staff can execute which tasks. Soft deleted.

permissionGroupMembers.notes: Junction table linking users to permission groups. No update operation; membership is added or removed. Hard deleted (no soft delete).

permissionGroupCategories.notes: Junction table linking task categories to permission groups. No update operation; category access is granted or revoked. Hard deleted (no soft delete).

executions.notes: Executions are immutable audit records. Created when a user triggers a task. Never updated via API after completion. Never deleted. The queue worker updates status and result fields during processing.

executionFiles.notes: Files uploaded by users or returned by engines for an execution. Created at execution time. Read for download. Expire automatically after 30 days (expiresAt). No soft delete.

scopeExceptions.exception: Client portal UI and ClientUser role interface are deferred to Phase 2. The client_workspaces and client_users data model and the client_user role enumeration are present in MVP for seamless Phase 2 delivery without schema refactoring.

GoHighLevel engine adapter is deferred to Phase 2. Make, Zapier, and custom webhook adapters are deferred to Phase 3. The Workflow Engine Adapter interface is implemented in MVP with n8n as the sole concrete adapter.
Multi-connection per organisation (multiple n8n instances) is deferred to Phase 2. MVP supports exactly one workflow engine connection per organisation.
Scheduled and batch task execution is deferred to Phase 3. MVP supports on-demand manual execution only.
Execution analytics, white-label branding, and public API are deferred to Phase 4.

performanceTargets.queueThroughput: Persistent FIFO queue, configurable worker concurrency
performanceTargets.apiResponseTime: < 200ms for read operations under normal load
performanceTargets.crossOrgLeakage: Zero cross-organisation data leakage enforced at query layer

complianceRequirements: All execution history retained indefinitely for audit purposes
Full error detail restricted to admin roles only
File storage with 30-day retention and secure presigned URLs for downloads
